Student Performance

Alina, Sasha, Katya, Sharvin

This is our original data. We will clean it up a bit to only include useful parts for our analysis.

##   Attendance_Percentages Stress_Rating
## 1                 0.5466          2.48
## 2                 0.5470          1.60
## 3                 0.5535          6.20
## 4                 0.5639          1.00
## 5                 0.5737          2.63
## 6                 0.5845          4.13

Graph of Points

Residual Analysis

*Note

When doing the residual analysis to check linear regression assumptions, we can see that out data has a megaphone shape, which means we don’t have constant variance. We will keep this in mind as a shortcoming of our analysis of the data.

Hypothesis Testing

Null hypothesis: there is no relationship between class attendance and stress levels (slope = 0)

Alternative hypothesis: there is a positive relationship between class attendance and stress levels (slope > 0)

Significance Level: 0.05

Result

Calculating test stat:

pt_est <- summary(stress_mod)$coefficients[2, 1]
se <- summary(stress_mod)$coefficients[2, 2]
n <- nrow(student_cleaned)

test_stat <- (pt_est - 0) / se

test_stat
## [1] 20.06167

Finding P-Value:

pt(test_stat, df = n-2, lower.tail = FALSE)
## [1] 8.837595e-76

Conclusion

Since our p-value is less than 0.05, we have enough evidence to conclude that there is a positive relationship between class attendance and stress levels of students!
